text: Close the laptop. Go home. Deploy Monday.
Ship it! The stars are aligned.
Maybe run the tests one more time. Just to be safe.
Have you considered deploying tomorrow instead?
YOLO. But like, responsible YOLO.
Deploy now. Strike while the CI is green.
Step away from the keyboard. Slowly.
Make sure your rollback plan is a plan and not a prayer.
Green light. Full speed ahead.
Maybe sleep on it? Your morning self is a better engineer.
Deploy with confidence. You've earned this.
Put the feature flag up and proceed with caution.
Announce the deploy in Slack first. Witnesses reduce recklessness.
Grab a buddy. Pair-deploying reduces the odds of solo disasters.
Check the on-call schedule. Make sure it's not you.
Send it. What's the worst that could happen? (Don't answer that.)
You have a 30-minute deploy window. Use it wisely.
Write the postmortem title now. It saves time later.
Double-check the target environment. Triple-check if it's production.
Perfect conditions. This is the deploy you've been training for.
Call your SRE. They love surprises. (They don't.)
The deploy window is optimal. Don't overthink it.
Maybe add a feature flag? Just a small one?
Pre-write the rollback command. Hope you don't need it.
Inform the team. Deploying in silence is deploying in danger.
Your monitoring dashboard should be your second screen right now.
Go. Ship. Celebrate. In that order.
The risk is low but your anxiety is high. That's normal.
Set a timer. If it's not deployed in 15 minutes, abort.
Remember: the deploy is just the beginning. The monitoring is the real work.
Open the incident channel preemptively. Just in case.
Wait for the next deploy window. Your future self will thank you.
Do a canary deploy first. Let 1% of users be the guinea pigs.
Deploy, then watch the metrics like a hawk for 15 minutes.
If your gut says no, listen to your gut. It's been through more deploys than you.
Confidence is high. Deploy with authority.
Notify downstream services. Nobody likes a surprise dependency change.
Take a deep breath. Click the button. Accept the consequences.
This is fine. Everything is fine. Deploy.
Schedule the deploy for tomorrow morning. Fresh eyes, fresh start.
Your deploy is small and focused. These are the ones that succeed.
Clear skies, green CI, full hearts. Can't lose.
You know what? Let the night shift handle this one.
Verify the health checks are actually checking health, not just returning 200.
Ask yourself: would I deploy this if I was on-call tonight?
Ship it. You've got tests, monitoring, and a rollback plan. That's the holy trinity.
Wait 10 minutes after the deploy before declaring victory.
Make sure the deploy doesn't require a database migration you forgot about.
Update the status page proactively. Transparency builds trust.
Merge with confidence. The tests are passing and the reviewers approved.
If the deploy takes more than 10 minutes, something is wrong. Abort.
Light a candle. Say a prayer. Click deploy.
The window is closing. Either deploy now or wait until next week.
Read the diff one more time. You'll catch something. You always do.
Let the automation handle it. That's what CI/CD is for.
Trust the process. But verify the process first.
Deploy to staging first. Yes, again.
Make sure someone else knows you're deploying. Solo deploys are solo disasters.
You're clear for takeoff. Don't forget the landing.
Bookmark the rollback docs. You won't need them. Probably.
